rows.text:
I'm not a fan of how British people write. Since I'm just a retarded American, by 'British', I mean anyone who lives in Europe. They spell things weird. They use different punctuation. They say "I went to University" or "I went to Hospital". I'm sorry, but that just ain't right. It's "the University" or "the hospital", you limey bastards.

That's really my biggest complaint about this story. All the Britishness kept shining through, and it distracted me from the story. Frankly, it pissed me off. Because the story is about a hot-ass whore in Liverpool. Sure, she isn't a whore by choice, but who is, really. Most girls don't long to be a whore when they grow up. They're thrust (heh, I said 'thrust') into the profession in one way or another.

That was the first, but not the last cliche in this story. Like, oh noes, will her non-John boyfriend find out that she's a whore? Of course he will. But will he just forgive her, so they can get on with their lovely life? Will they fall in love, get married, have little whore babies? No way. She's a whore, for Christ's sake.

But she's also practically royalty. Her father is a lord. Can you believe that? A lord has a whore for a daughter. Sweet. I bet he's really proud. Probably sent her to the best whore school. Hired her a good whore tutor. Helped her with her whore homework. Ok

rows.text:
It's been a long time since I've seen a movie this craptastic. The dialogue was passable--at least initially (it got crappy really quickly)--but the acting was horrible. Even some of the scene setups for said dialogue were just pathetically too coincidental. The crappy acting from all characters pretty much destroyed any interest I might have had 10 minutes into the movie.

Wait! There's more!

The entire intro felt like something out of the '80s or '90s with all the crappy music, credits, and nothing interesting to watch except a bunch of names that don't mean a thing to me because I haven't yet seen anything worth watching (already I'm bored and losing interest). I guess the director and editors didn't get the memo that that style went out a while back. And, yes, I did check some other movies from the same year to make sure I wasn't misremembering, but none of the ones I pulled up had the same crappy music credits sequence at the beginning of the movie. They had an actual story going on with the credits, if not dialogue.

There's more!

If you're still reading this, you'll be amused to know that this crappy intro, with crappy acting, and crappy music is then followed by one of the crappiest chase scenes I've ever had the misfortune to watch. rows.text:
I loved this movie, but there were four problems that made it difficult to love.

First, the character, Mary Kelly, is a whore who apparently never works. You see her friends working, and even witness the transaction between a whore and her trick. But Mary Kelly is clean and nice and somehow manages to make a living as a whore without having any customers. I mean, I understand that one of the main characters can't be depicted as this disgusting, filthy, diseased whore, because no one would want to identify with her (whether they actually did or not is another story), but the way they sanitized it was ridiculous.

Second, the ending was just plain stupid. They set it up so that this poor little French whore is killed in Mary Kelly's place (I don't care if I ruined it for you), and that Mary Kelly escapes back to Ireland where she raises a little orphaned girl. This made me so angry I couldn't stand it. First of all, the little French whore was just as sweet and nice as Mary Kelly was, but they make it look like it's somehow OK that the French whore was killed - it's NOT OK to kill off a whore just because she isn't your focus. That in no way reduces the horror of the act itself. ("Besides, there's no reason to kill [whores] - most of them are already dead inside.") It was a stupid and endlessly irritating plot "twist." I'll bet some Hollywood dim-wi
rows.title: Problems to get past

I've been collecting coins and using Littleton archive quality folders for a long time now, and this particular folder is different than most of the others I've used in that Littleton tried to get cheap and squeeze three rows of quarters on every page into one clear covering front and back....and this is a really stupid and crappy cheapskate design trying to save on another single row sheet.
So why does this totally suck? When you place the quarters in the middle row of three, there isn't sufficient top and bottom pressure from the clear sheets to keep the quarters in their holes - which don't seem to be the consistent sized so some of the quarters just fall right through before you ever get to this other problem., so they fall out and disappear - stuck somewhere between the page's top covering and the cheap crap cardboard they didn't size correctly to keep the quarters in place...so now, several of my uncirculated quarters I had on pages with this idiot design have become scratched and marred, with their once pristine and clean surfaces looking like I got my coins out of a vending machine...and I'm pissed about. If you want to scratch up and mar your pristine coins, buy this piece of trash and you're on your way. This folder is pure junk. I bought this to replace a worn out Whitman folder that also used transparent sheets - but their design wasn't cheap stupid crap and it

rows.text: I usually don't write reviews, specifically negative ones, but this needs to be said and consumers need to hear and heed warning. I bought this littler box hoping it would make my life easier; cleaning cat poop and scooping piss daily is a pain in the @ss. I saw the reviews on this specific one and got sold on the idea that it was "scoop free" since it was a sifting box. Nope you will still need to scoop poop and piss that is clinging on to the sifter AND now work is twice that since you have poop and piss caked in the sifter's small openings. Anyone thinking, after reading this, that I allowed the litter box to get unkempt for days or weeks at a time- the is answer is no. Even with daily maintaining of the box, piss and poop builds up in the nooks of the sifter. Oh! and to add to this, when my cat pees (she's a sprayer), the urine is able to leak outside of the freaking box.... so now I have to deal with a pissy wall.  I'm out 16 bucks... literally a crappy situations, pissy wall and a pissed off cat owner.

Crazy Bitch is the 5th book in the series of Biker Bitches by Jamie Begley, this book is based on Crazy Bitch and Calder.

If you've already read Jamie's books you'll already know who Crazy Bitch as she is part of the badass, psychotic, loyal biker bitches group. Calder is a recovering drug addict, brother to Stud and the President of The Blue Horsemen.

Years ago Crazy Bitch decided to take a chance with Calder, he ruined it and since then she's stayed away from him. This book is about second chances, family, love, loyalty, acceptance, forgiveness and friendships.
We get to see and learn more about both characters that we hadn't known before, we do get to know why Crazy Bitch is called Crazy Bitch and get to learn her real name. I'm so excited to read more about Gavin, I hear he is going to be getting a book, just depends when as the next biker book we will be getting will be about Rider. 😍😄

rows.text:
When I saw that there was a male-version of 'Skinny Bitch' coming out, I was excited. I read 'Skinny Bitch' and, despite much of the criticism, found it to be one of those life-changing books. It convinced me that going vegetarian was the way to go and that trying to be vegan was also a worthwhile venture. By reading 'Skinny Bitch' and following some of the advice it contained, I managed to lose a good 40 or 50 pounds, with the help of exercise in addition to the nutrition tips.

Now, 'Skinny Bastard'. Here's my advice; if you're a guy and you were too embarrassed to be seen toting around and flipping through 'Skinny Bitch', this is your book. If you read 'Skinny Bitch', don't expect to get much more out of this book. Wisely, I read through some of the chapters and flipped through the book at a local bookstore and was astonished to find that someone had the audacity to pretty much take 'Skinny Bitch', pack in a few more pages, change the packaging, make it 'speak' more to men and call it a new book. This is a blatant rip-off of one book pretty much attempting to spawn a franchise.

In summary, good material, but read 'Skinny Bitch' or 'Skinny Bastard', not both. 'Skinny Bastard' is for men and looks like it may have a few additional tid-bits that its female equivalent doesn't. Otherwise, don't waste your time and money on a book you've probably already have
rows.title: 'Skinny Bastard' nothing more than a re-packaged 'Skinny Bitchy'

rows.text:
I'm puzzled by how this book became such a huge hit. It's the fast food of literature - cheap, bloated and easy. The only difference is fast food tastes good. Don't try eating the book. I tried and it left me with indigestion. I figured if I didn't like the book that I'd may as well improve my fiber intake. Bad idea.

This tome of flotsam floated into my consciousness at the urging of a friend. I don't normally read books like this, but she was enrapt, completely thrilled by it. Since I'm a big reader and wanted to see what the fuss was about, I gave it a go. And if I didn't like it, well it's good intestinal ruffage for me.

The story I read followed this basic pattern and dialogue, all involving the overused word "crap" and it's conjugations:

"He's looking at me. Crap. I'm looking at him. Double crap. He cocked his head (also overused) to the side. Triple crap. Oh my, his eyes are so grey (this is repeated about 670,983 times). Quadrupled crappity crapping crap playing craps while crapping on the crappy crapping crapper."

The main protagonist, Anastasia Steele, likes to say "crap" a lot. It made me have to go to the bathroom. And I ATE this book?

I wonder again what makes everyone praise this thing and why it is such a hit. It's incredibly pedestrian, sophomoric and trite. Why does this sell? I feel like the truly good book
rows.title: Variations on the word "crap".

ALWAYS shut your fuel cock off when parking your vehicle.

rows.text:
Pros:
- Correct size and Thread to replace the defective breakers on the hose cocks.
- Material Quality was the same as the OEM parts.
- Easy to install if you do not destroy the threads on the hose cock when removing the vacuum breaker.
- Comes with rubber gasket so no other parts needed to install.
- Very fast delivery.

Cons:
- While less expensive than the OEM parts, still somewhat pricey for a basic turned metal piece. The price was of this part was the lowest I could find so it is really not a negative.

I purchased a building that had the hose cocks with external vacuum breakers which were broken and leaking. Replacement vacuum breakers are expensive, prone to failure, and have a tendency to leak. I took the defective vacuum breakers off and replaced them with these pass through adapters. The water flow rate is much improved and there are no leaks to waste water.

Be sure to choose the correct size adapter to fit the thread size and pattern on your hose cock. Also, some vacuum breakers are pinned or have screws attaching the vacuum breaker to the hose cock which need to be removed before the vacuum breaker can be removed. If you just try to unscrew those from the hose cock you can destroy the threads or destroy the hose cock entirely.
rows.title: JL Brand SM01 Vacuum Breaker Adapter - Same Product Just Less Expensive

Grade: F

L/C Ratio: 65/35
(This means I estimate the author devoted 65% of his effort to creating a literary work of art and 35% of his effort to creating a commercial bestseller.)

Thematic Breakdown:
30% - Collapse of American life
20% - Failing relationships
15% - Parenthood
15% - Comedy
10% - Office politics
10% - Visual arts

I can't remember ever being this frustrated with a novel. The opening chapters of The Ask had me laughing obnoxiously, as Lipstye introduces the character of Milo Burke with an authorial voice that has more bite and punch than anything else you'll find in contemporary fiction.

Then you, the reader, gets to know Milo Burke. And you discover quite quickly that he is a dick. But it doesn't stop there. His wife is a dick. His son's babysitter is a dick. His boss is a dick. His coworkers are all dicks. His rich friend from college is a dick. And that rich friend's illegitimate son, who lost his legs fighting in Iraq, even manages to be a dick.

Despite the total lack of likable characters, I was still holding out some hope for The Ask. Lipstye appeared to be on the verge of saying something profound about post-9/11 American society. Then I arrived at the quote that appears at the bottom of this post (feel free to go read it right now), and I lost what little respect I ha
rows.title: Grade: F

I have been through three different switches from two different companies and they all fail somewhere between a few weeks and several months after purchase. The problem is no reputable company makes these things, so you are stuck with a crap manufacturer with no engineering skill. These things are a tremendous benefit to have at your desk if you have more than one computer to switch between, but the product quality suuuuuuucccks. To spare myself some insult to injury, I am just copying this same review to the three of them that have failed. Any positive review either a) is fake, b) did not wait long enough, or c) did the math and decided that it is worth the cost to replace every few months (and honestly, it might be, because it is a pita to switch out the USB cables constantly). People love to delude themselves into believing that their experience will fall in with those of the positive reviewers: good luck.
PS--the positive of this crap vs other crap is that this crap has the button that you can put next to your mouse (or wherever) making it easier to switch between devices. If you need a crappy switch, I recommend the crap with the separate button over the crap with the button on the USB box.

We are all familiar with the visual of the crotchety, old man shouting at cars driving 3 mph over the speed limit in his neighborhood. When we see him we may think to ourselves, "Geeze guy, settle down, you're going to pull something..." before it dawns on us that before long, WE will be that crotchety old man or woman. For me anyway, there is this realization that given how jaded I already am at 35 about all of life's little annoyances that full-on crochetyness is an inevitability...and that it isn't all that far off.

What makes me particularly salty are all the CRAP products out there that CRAP OUT on you before they should. When did it become a bleeping foregone conclusion that 80% of consumer products will turn to worthless CRAP in mere months? $200 for this? CRAP in a heartbeat. $120 for that? Yep, turned to CRAP in lightning speed. Sure, the more you spend the lesser the probability the product will be CRAP but there is no hard and fast rule where "you spend X and you're guaranteed to avoid CRAP." It's a poor commentary on the whole human race in my opinion...but I digress.

Every so often however, in the midst of thousands upon thousands of CRAP products here and CRAP products there, you come across a gem. THIS BLENDER, LADIES AND GENTLEMAN, IS THAT GEM. I think I paid $29 for this thing when I bought it a few months ago. I figured meh...
rows.title: I'm stunned...

rows.text:
In what really should have been the second disc to "Jackass Number Two", Jackass 2.5 features behind-the-scenes, deleted segments, and interviews of the Jackass crew. That's pretty much it. All 64 minutes. Sure, some of the segments are funny, like the "Beauty and the Beast" segment and the one with Johnny Knoxville getting a prostate exam, but really, nothing that constitutes paying $20 or more.

Special Features-
*The Making of Jackass 2.5- Seems really redundant at this point, but it's more interviews and deleted stunts
*The Making of Jackass The Game- A look at the Jackass gang doing motion capture for their game. Pretty funny.
*Bonus Segments and Stunts- More stunts and segments from filming Number Two and their MTV premiere of Jackass The Movie.
*A plug for jackassworld.com
*A photo gallery
*Previews for Wildboyz seasons 3 & 4 on DVD, The Heartbreak Kid, and Rob & Big seasons 1 & 2 on DVD.

Overall, I don't think anyone should buy this unless they really need to complete their Jackass video collection. Now, I am a fan (I got both movies, and the box set), and I felt a little ripped off when I actually watched it. Would be a better idea to buy it if it was around $10 or so.
rows.title: Why, Jackass? Why?
